dimension_notes_summary.cross_module_architecture: Clean 3-layer dependency direction: src/segments/ -> src/ -> src/campaigns/. Segments import from core (types, colors, config, effort), campaigns import from core (types), and core (layout.ts) imports
dimension_notes_summary.high_level_elegance: 14-file codebase with 3 directories maps cleanly to domain capabilities: src/ (core infrastructure: types, colors, config, layout, entry points), src/segments/ (rendering units: context, model, git, c
dimension_notes_summary.convention_outlier: All functions use camelCase consistently across all 3 directories (campaigns/, src/, segments/). No naming convention drift.
dimension_notes_summary.error_consistency: Two distinct error strategies are used: (1) silent swallow with fallback behavior (index.ts, config.ts, effort.ts, git.ts catch blocks), (2) log-and-exit in CLI (cli.ts init() catches, logs with conso
dimension_notes_summary.naming_quality: Segment names (contextSegment, modelSegment, gitSegment, campaignSegment, usageSegment) clearly identify their rendering domain
dimension_notes_summary.abstraction_fitness: The Segment interface has five concrete implementations (context, model, git, campaign, usage), all with meaningfully different render() and enabled() implementations -- genuine polymorphism
dimension_notes_summary.dependency_health: package.json has zero production dependencies -- the project uses only Node.js built-in modules (child_process, fs, path, os)
dimension_notes_summary.low_level_elegance: layout.ts render() is 120 lines mixing brute-force assignment enumeration, row materialization, and rendering -- three distinct concerns in one function body
dimension_notes_summary.mid_level_elegance: Segment interface defines a clean, uniform contract: id, priority, enabled(data), render(data, width) -> Block. All 5 segments implement this identically, making the handoff from layout.ts to segments
dimension_notes_summary.test_strategy: Zero test files exist in the entire repository: no *.test.ts, no *.spec.ts, no test/ directory, no __tests__ directory.
dimension_notes_summary.api_surface_coherence: The primary public API is render(data: StatusLineData, termWidth: number, config: StatusBlocksConfig): string in layout.ts — 3 clear, typed parameters with an obvious return type.
dimension_notes_summary.authorization_consistency: This is a terminal status-line renderer — a CLI tool that reads JSON from stdin, formats it with ANSI colors, and writes to stdout. It has no route handlers, no HTTP endpoints, no authentication middl

dimension_notes_summary.ai_generated_debt: Comment ratio is 5.2% -- very low, with no restating comments found. Comments explain WHY (e.g., '// Claude Code's outer paddingX: 2 on each side', '// Can be negative on early Saturday before peak-en
dimension_notes_summary.incomplete_migration: Pure TypeScript codebase -- all 14 production files are .ts with no .js source files. ESM-only with 'type: module' in package.json.
dimension_notes_summary.package_organization: Three directories with clear purpose: src/ (7 files, orchestration + utilities), src/segments/ (5 files, rendering units), src/campaigns/ (2 files, promotion logic).
dimension_notes_summary.initialization_coupling: No module-level code that depends on another module having been imported first. All imports are pure type or function imports.
dimension_notes_summary.design_coherence: Functions are generally focused with single responsibilities. Campaign engine decomposes into resolveWeekendState/resolvePeakState/resolveOffPeakState. Layout decomposes into boxify/renderRow/findOpti
dimension_notes_summary.contract_coherence: Segment.enabled() and Segment.render() contracts are consistent: enabled() returns boolean indicating whether the segment should be rendered, render() returns a Block. All 5 implementations honor this
dimension_notes_summary.logic_clarity: Control flow in most functions is direct and traceable: getActiveCampaign() iterates campaigns with clear early returns for each state, segments have simple if/else for data availability.
dimension_notes_summary.type_safety: All public functions have explicit return type annotations or inferable return types. Segment interface enforces typed render() and enabled() signatures.

review_issue_summaries.summary: layout.ts hardcodes all segment imports -- adding a segment requires editing layout.ts
Catch blocks inconsistently document their rationale -- git.ts line 27 has bare catch
findOptimalAssignment uses dense brute-force iteration with single-letter variables across 55 lines
resolveEffort has 3 levels of nested try-catch creating unnecessary depth
effort.test.ts has tautological assertions that accept any return value and never fail
Campaign engine's timezone-aware date arithmetic lacks peak boundary edge case tests
cli.ts (92 LOC) has zero test coverage for init, preview, and help commands
Segment render() allocWidth parameter is only used by 1 of 5 implementations
model.ts render() still has high local variable density despite spreadLine() extraction
promoSegment.enabled() and render() both call getActiveCampaign() independently, risking state divergence
layout.ts applies an additional 5-char offset for row 1 beyond the existing INK_PADDING
JSON.parse(input) as StatusLineData casts external stdin input without runtime validation

dimension_notes_summary.cross_module_architecture: Dependency direction is clean: segments/ imports from src/ (types, colors, effort, campaigns), src/ imports from segments/ only in layout.ts for segment registration, campaigns/ imports only from src/
dimension_notes_summary.high_level_elegance: Top-level decomposition maps cleanly to domain capabilities: src/ for core pipeline (index, layout, config, colors, types, effort, cli), src/segments/ for rendering units, src/campaigns/ for promotion
dimension_notes_summary.convention_outlier: Consistent camelCase naming across all directories: campaigns/ (8 camelCase), src/ (10 camelCase), segments/ (6 camelCase) with zero snake_case drift
dimension_notes_summary.error_consistency: Consistent try-catch-swallow pattern at I/O boundaries: index.ts (stdin parse), config.ts (file read), effort.ts (transcript/settings read), git.ts (git commands) — all use try-catch with silent swall
dimension_notes_summary.naming_quality: Function names are domain-specific and intent-revealing: formatTokens, tildeDir, spreadLine, formatCountdown, formatResetTime, miniBar, getTimeInTz, resolveEffort, getActiveCampaign, visibleLength, pa
dimension_notes_summary.abstraction_fitness: The Segment interface has genuine polymorphic value — 5 implementations, used by layout.ts for uniform rendering. Not a one-implementation ceremony interface.
dimension_notes_summary.dependency_health: Zero production dependencies in package.json — the tool runs entirely on Node.js built-in modules (child_process, fs, path, os) and Intl APIs
dimension_notes_summary.low_level_elegance: findOptimalAssignment (layout.ts lines 74-147) is a dense 73-line combinatorial algorithm with single-letter loop variables (encoded, combo, block, row) and pre-allocated buffers — effective but requi
dimension_notes_summary.mid_level_elegance: The data flow pipeline is clean: stdin JSON -> parse/validate -> loadConfig -> detect terminal width -> render(data, width, config) -> stdout. Each step has clear inputs and outputs.
dimension_notes_summary.test_strategy: 6 test files exist covering: colors.test.ts (comprehensive utility tests), config.test.ts (env var override tests), effort.test.ts (transcript parsing tests), layout.test.ts (assignment algorithm + re
dimension_notes_summary.api_surface_coherence: The Segment interface provides a consistent API shape across all 5 implementations: id (string), priority (number), enabled(data) -> boolean, render(data, allocWidth) -> Block
dimension_notes_summary.authorization_consistency: This is a local CLI tool that reads stdin and writes to stdout — it has no network endpoints, no route handlers, no API endpoints, and no authentication requirements
dimension_notes_summary.ai_generated_debt: Very low comment ratio (5.2%) — comments are sparse and purposeful, not restating
dimension_notes_summary.incomplete_migration: All source files are TypeScript (.ts) — no mixed JS/TS files indicating incomplete TypeScript migration
dimension_notes_summary.package_organization: Three directories with clear purpose: src/ (7 core files, avg fan-in 4.4), src/segments/ (5 segment implementations, avg fan-in 1.6), src/campaigns/ (2 campaign files, avg fan-in 1.5)
dimension_notes_summary.initialization_coupling: No module-level code that depends on import ordering — all modules define functions and constants that are independent of import sequence
dimension_notes_summary.design_coherence: Functions are generally focused: each segment render function has a single responsibility (produce styled lines for its domain), campaign state resolvers each handle one temporal scenario
dimension_notes_summary.contract_coherence: Return types match all code paths: getActiveCampaign() returns CampaignStatus | null and all paths (upcoming, ended/continue, weekend, peak, off-peak, null) are covered
dimension_notes_summary.logic_clarity: No identical if/else branches — all branches produce distinct outputs (e.g., promo.ts state branches each produce different line1/line2 combinations)
dimension_notes_summary.type_safety: TypeScript strict mode is used consistently — all functions have return type annotations or inferred types
